footerTagline: Those who take Open Source seriously help shape it.

dltLecture.metaTitle: DLT & Digital Trust Lecture - Open Elements
dltLecture.metaDescription: Since 2023 Hendrik Ebbers has been offering the lecture 'Distribution Ledger Technology and Digital Trust'.
dltLecture.title: DLT & Digital Trust Lecture
dltLecture.underlineAlt: Graphical element that underlines the text
dltLecture.intro: Since 2023 <hendrik>Hendrik Ebbers</hendrik> has been offering the lecture ‘Distribution Ledger Technology and Digital Trust’. Currently, the lecture is held as a teaching assignment at <oth>OTH Regensburg</oth>.
dltLecture.summary: The lecture deals with the basics of Distributed Ledger Technologies (DLT) and provides the necessary knowledge to develop DLT-based systems.
dltLecture.excerptHeading: Excerpt of the lecture from the course catalog of the OTH Regensburg
dltLecture.excerptIntro: The lecture provides a basic understanding of distributed ledger technologies. In addition to the technical basics of cryptocurrencies, more advanced approaches such as smart contracts and NFTs. The following points are discussed in detail:
bulletPoints: History and background of Bitcoin and distributed ledger technologies
Implementation of blockchains and mining of cryptocurrencies
Possible applications of distributed ledger technologies
Use and integration of public distributed ledger technologies
Smart contracts and the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)
dltLecture.quoteTextFallback: The lecture gave me a clear and practical introduction to a complex topic. The hands-on parts especially helped me apply what I learned.

altTexts.underline: Graphical element that underlines the text

hero.description: We believe that openness and transparency create substantial value for companies, projects, and individuals. Our goal is to support our partners in establishing important concepts such as Open Source and Open Data. This strengthens core aspects of IT, such as standards and security automatically within the company. Through our work, we also promote active engagement in the IT scene and beyond.

homeLanding.tagline: Those who take Open Source seriously help shape it.

hero.body: For us, this means more than curation: we maintain the Java components you rely on every day. We help shape the open standards that sustainably define the future. We are building an open-source product family.
hero.imageAlt: People collaboratively shaping an open-source ecosystem

business.title: What we do.
business.intro: Three business areas, one shared idea: each strengthens the others, and together they advance transparency, open source, and sustainable digitalization.

supportCare.headline: Online Java infrastructure support.
supportCare.body: We work with Kubernetes, Maven, Jenkins, and typical Java open-source tooling. We support teams with quality assurance, build pipelines, and sustainable operations.
supportCare.imageAlt: Support specialist with a chat symbol

digitalTrust.headline: Authenticity through verifiable information.
digitalTrust.body: With open technical means, we create trustworthy digital processes. This includes identities, proofs, and traceable communication.

labs.headline: Software for the next generation of digital applications.
labs.body: In the labs, we develop technological foundations for decentralized infrastructure, sustainable systems, and modern digital tools.

distinguish.body: Long-term strategies, open standards, and genuine commitment in the open-source world. That is what distinguishes us from traditional agencies.
distinguish.cta: Open Elements is active in these areas
distinguish.imageAlt: Open-source network between Europe and Germany
distinguish.summary: Many networks work together on foundations for open source, digital identity, and sustainable infrastructure.
distinguish.summaryCta: Open Elements is active in these areas

eclipse.body: In our modern labs, we develop the next generation of digital tools.

linux.body: In our modern labs, we develop.

values.body: Long-term strategies, open standards, and genuine commitment in the open-source world. That is what distinguishes us from traditional agencies.
values.imageAlt: People building an open digital universe together

openness.title: Openness as responsibility.
openness.body: We develop solutions transparently and traceably. Collaboration and knowledge sharing are at the center of our daily work.

community.title: Community before products.
community.body: Sustainable software is created by strong communities. That is why we invest long-term in open collaboration.

depth.title: Depth before breadth.
depth.body: We focus on selected technologies and build real expertise within them.

contact.body: Do you have a concrete project where open source plays a central role? Then let's think it through together.
contact.imageAlt: Team working together on a growing tree
contact.awardsIntro: Those who take Open Source seriously help shape it.

openKnowledge.title: Open Knowledge
openKnowledge.intro: The open availability of well-prepared knowledge is essential for us, therefore we share a large part of our know-how in
openKnowledge.outro1: even though – or better said – because we earn our money with expertise.
openKnowledge.outro2: The free accessibility of knowledge is very important to us, even outside of our area of expertise. Therefore, we act as a sponsor of

openEvents.title: Open Events
openEvents.description2: and enable via sponsorship, free meetups and physical events in the metropolitan region Rhine-Ruhr. We actively participate in the management of the
openEvents.description4: initiative. In doing so, we support the offering of free or cost-covering events in the German-speaking Java and IT scene.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description1: Open Source is a matter close to our heart. We believe that software must be largely openly available in order to advance society – by, among other things, establishing open standards or enabling manufacturer independence and transparent security audits.
openSource.description2: Therefore, we are a member of the
openSource.description3: and also actively work with others on key projects in the Java ecosystem, such as
openSource.description4: . Furthermore, we support small OS projects and develop core components ourselves as open source software.

openDoors.title: Open Doors
openDoors.box1: This puts us on an equal footing with interested applicants, existing and new customers, potential partner companies, and people for whom our main topics are of interest.
openDoors.box2: We always have an open ear for others.
openDoors.box3End: to chat with us about topics like open source and Java or discuss technical issues with us.

openOffice.title: Open Office
openOffice.percentageText: Every employee has a 100% free choice of a workplace with us.
openOffice.description1: We want to offer all employees the most comfortable integration of working time into their individual life.
openOffice.description2: In a home office or anywhere in the world in a co-working space: for us, open office is not a status symbol but a lived value.

calendly.description: The easiest way to get in touch with us is by booking a free 30 min slot in our open calendar. By doing so we can start a discussion and talk about your concerns, interest or problems regarding any of our focused topics like Open Source and Java.

email.intro: Next to this you can send us a mail at

page.title: Support & Care
page.subtitle: Sustainable support for open source software
page.intro1: Support & Care is a program for the sustainable further development, stabilization and support of open source software (OSS). Open Elements works with both the industry and the open source community to ensure the quality, independence and development of critical open source projects and components.
page.intro2: With Support & Care, Open Elements offers a solution for critical projects so that the products can be used without hesitation in a professional environment. Our open source strategy aims to improve the direction of the projects through financial support and active collaboration and to prevent underfunding, poor maintenance and security of this critical OSS.
page.services: Through Support & Care, we provide comprehensive services to ensure the performance and security of your applications. Our services go beyond classic troubleshooting and promote the sustainable improvement of your infrastructure.

maven.title: Support & Care for Apache Maven™
maven.description: Support & Care for Apache Maven™ strengthens the future of the Java ecosystem through sustainable funding and transparent development. Secure the long-term stability and continued development of Apache Maven.

temurin.title: Eclipse Temurin Support & Care
temurin.description: Professional support for Eclipse Temurin, the most widely used Java runtime environment with over 20 million downloads per month. Ensure the long-term functionality of your Java applications.

maven.intro1: Open Elements has launched a major initiative with its program Support & Care for Apache Maven™, helping to secure a central tool of the Java ecosystem. With funding from the {sta}, sustainable support for Apache Maven is enabled for the first time—one of the world's most widely used open-source projects.
maven.intro2: We now offer companies and organizations the opportunity to participate in this important undertaking. By subscribing to a Support & Care subscription, you contribute to securing the long-term stability and continued development of Apache Maven. Your support helps finance critical tasks such as security updates, bug fixes, and the development of new features.
maven.intro3: In addition to supporting community-driven development, a Support & Care subscription allows you to directly access our experts and maintainers to prioritize bug fixes or assist you with other topics related to Apache Maven™.
maven.subscriptionTitle: Our Subscription Model for Apache Maven™
maven.subscriptionSubtitle: We offer three different packages tailored to the needs of various user groups. We are happy to help you determine which package best suits your requirements.
maven.closingStatement: We look forward to shaping the future of Apache Maven together with you!
maven.staTitle: Support from the Sovereign Tech Agency
maven.sta1: The Sovereign Tech Agency (STA) is a funding program of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action dedicated to the sustainable support of open-source software. The program aims to secure critical digital infrastructures in the long term and strengthen the resilience of the European technology ecosystem. Projects funded by the STA must be of significant importance to the economy and society while following sustainable development practices.
maven.sta2: Since 2024, the STA has been investing in the Support & Care for Apache Maven™ project, enabling sustainable cost-sharing between public funding and commercial contributions. The STA's investment lays the foundation for a sustainable future of the project and the entire Java ecosystem. However, broad support from the community and industry is essential to secure Apache Maven in the long term.
maven.whoWorksTitle: Who Works on Support & Care for Apache Maven™?
maven.whoWorks: Employees of Open Elements as well as external developers and maintainers work together on Support & Care for Apache Maven™. While project management and coordination are handled by Open Elements, development is carried out by both our engineers and long-standing external maintainers of Apache Maven. This close collaboration with the Apache Software Foundation and the active Apache Maven community ensures that the project evolves in line with user needs.
maven.modelTitle: A Model Project for Open Source
maven.model1: With Support & Care for Apache Maven™, Open Elements is taking a strong step toward sustainable support for open-source projects. This program serves as a pilot within the Support & Care initiative, which aims to invest in additional critical open-source components.
maven.model2: The concept combines public funding, commercial support, transparent financial management, and active community involvement. It demonstrates how essential open-source projects can be made future-proof while fostering innovation — a model that can inspire similar initiatives across the open-source ecosystem.
maven.trademark: Apache™ and Apache Maven™ are trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.
maven.footnote1: Unused support hours expire at the end of the month — in this case, your contribution directly supports the continued development of the respective open-source components.
maven.footnote2: Business days are Monday through Friday, excluding public holidays in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.
maven.footnote3: Our helpdesk uses a GDPR-compliant, EU-hosted solution. Customers receive individual accounts, and communication is available in both German and English.
maven.footnote4: Our experts are developers and technical staff who are directly involved in the respective OSS projects (e.g., as committers or maintainers).
maven.footnote5: We use Zoom for webinars and individual meetings.

basic.name: Basic Subscription
basic.bullet1: 4 hours of support per month included<sup>1</sup>
basic.bullet3: Support requests via helpdesk in German and English<sup>3</sup>
basic.bullet4: 10% discount on every additional support hour
basic.bullet5: Quarterly webinar directly with the experts<sup>4,5</sup>

standard.name: Standard Subscription
standard.bullet1: 8 hours of support per month included<sup>1</sup>
standard.bullet3: Support requests via helpdesk in German and English<sup>3</sup>
standard.bullet4: 15% discount on every additional support hour
standard.bullet5: Quarterly webinar directly with the experts<sup>4,5</sup>

premium.name: Premium Subscription
premium.bullet1: 8 hours of support per month included<sup>1</sup>
premium.bullet3: Support requests via helpdesk and hotline in German and English<sup>3</sup>
premium.bullet4: 15% discount on every additional support hour
premium.bullet5: Quarterly webinar directly with the experts<sup>4,5</sup>

landingpage.heroParagraph: Professional maintenance, security updates and long term support for the most business-critical open source components in the Java ecosystem — directly from the maintainers.
landingpage.heroBody: Modern software consists of over 70% open source components. From 2027, the Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) will hold manufacturers responsible for 100% of their software — including all OSS dependencies. Support & Care secures the foundation of your Java applications: from the runtime environment to build tools and testing strategy.
landingpage.problemP1: A simple Java project with Spring Boot brings over 70 transitive dependencies — most of them open source. Your individual code is just the tip of the iceberg. Underneath lie runtime environments, build tools, logging frameworks, test libraries and utility libraries that actually carry the operation of your application.
landingpage.problemHighlight: 70% of software is based on open source and is therefore outside your direct control.
landingpage.problemP2: These foundational components are often maintained by individual developers in their spare time. At the same time, they carry the majority of technical risks: security vulnerabilities, transitive dependencies, missing documentation and compliance responsibility.
landingpage.problemBullet1: Vulnerabilities in foundational components often go unnoticed until it is too late
landingpage.problemBullet2: Framework support alone does not protect against gaps in the foundation — Log4Shell clearly demonstrated this
landingpage.problemBullet3: The CRA will hold you liable for the entire software supply chain from 2027
landingpage.componentsIntro: Support & Care specifically covers the five most business-critical open source foundational components of the Java ecosystem. Together, they form the technical chain of trust for virtually every Java application.

temurin.name: Eclipse Temurin — Java Runtime
temurin.desc:
Leading vendor-independent OpenJDK distribution worldwide
Over 500,000 downloads per day
TCK-certified, AQAvit-verified, community-driven

maven.name: Apache Maven — Build & Dependency Management
maven.desc:
Over 75% of all Java projects use Maven
Approx. 2 billion downloads annually

junit.name: JUnit — Test Framework
junit.desc:
Over 1 billion downloads per month
Approx. 85% market share in the Java ecosystem

log4j.name: Apache Log4j — Logging
log4j.desc:
Approx. 76% of all Java applications use Log4j
Business-critical for logging, monitoring and error analysis

commons.name: Apache Commons — Standard Libraries
commons.desc:
Approx. 49% of Java developers actively use Apache Commons
Modular collection: Lang, IO, Collections and more

landingpage.componentsHighlight: In short: The essential foundation of the technical chain of trust for your Java applications.
landingpage.layersIntro: Java applications can be divided into three layers:
landingpage.pyramidAlt: The 3 layers of a Java application
landingpage.layer1Desc: Your individual business and domain logic code. This layer is highly valuable but relatively small in scope — it builds on frameworks and foundational technologies.
landingpage.layer2Desc: Spring Boot, Quarkus, Jakarta EE and others. Commercial support from the respective vendors is widely available for this layer.
landingpage.layer3: Foundational Components — This is where Support & Care steps in.
landingpage.layer3Desc: Runtime environment, build and dependency management, standard libraries, logging and test frameworks. These components are used in virtually every Java project — yet professional support has been largely unavailable until now.
landingpage.layersHighlight: Framework support alone is not enough. The Log4Shell vulnerability demonstrated: A critical security flaw in a foundational component can affect millions of applications — despite up-to-date framework updates. Support & Care closes exactly this gap.
landingpage.servicesIntro: All services are delivered directly by the maintainers and committers of the supported projects — not by a downstream support team.

lts.name: Long Term Support (LTS)
lts.desc: Continued support for the most important versions to help you better plan and organize your updates. You never have to rely on insecure or unmaintained versions.

security.name: Security Updates & Bugfixes
security.desc: Early information and notifications about vulnerabilities and patches. Fast response times through direct access to the developers.

documentation.name: Documentation & Transparency
documentation.desc: Support with SBOM strategies and technical documentation — in German or English. Transparent traceability of all changes.

workshops.name: Workshops & Consulting
workshops.desc: Direct exchange with the maintainers and committers of the projects — in German or English. Individual consulting on migration, best practices and architectural decisions.

webinars.name: Regular Webinars & Status Updates
webinars.desc: Quarterly webinars on current security risks, important version changes, best practice recommendations and concrete impacts on your OSS supply chain.

customBuilds.name: Custom Builds & Tooling
customBuilds.desc: Tailored implementations directly by the maintainers — from special build configurations to individualized tooling solutions.

landingpage.craTitle: Prepared for the Cyber Resilience Act
landingpage.craP1: From 2027, manufacturers will be responsible for 100% of their software under the Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — including all open source dependencies. This covers patch times, vulnerability management, documentation and long-term maintainability. Open Elements acts as an open source steward and actively shapes the regulatory framework. As a founding member of the Open Regulatory Compliance Working Group (ORC WG) of the Eclipse Foundation, we work together with leading open source foundations, major technology companies and EU representatives on concrete specifications and practical guidelines for CRA implementation.
landingpage.craBulletTitle: Support & Care specifically helps you with:
landingpage.craBullet6: Prospectively: CRA-compliant attestations for supported projects
landingpage.craHighlight: Open Elements is a founding member of the ORC WG and works directly on the best practices that define how CRA compliance for open source software is implemented. This expertise flows directly into Support & Care.
landingpage.containersTitle: Hardened Containers for Government and Public Administration
landingpage.containersP1: This is also Support & Care: Hardened containers for the German public administration.
landingpage.containersP2: Open Elements belongs to an exclusive group of organizations authorized to provide hardened container images for container.gov.de — alongside the Center for Digital Sovereignty (ZenDiS) and the German Federal Foreign Office. For Support & Care customers, this means: The hardened Eclipse Temurin images for all current Java LTS versions (Java 11, 17, 21, 25+) are included in the service scope. Verified, signed and continuously checked against current vulnerability databases.
landingpage.containersBullet3: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for full transparency
landingpage.containersBullet5: Minimized attack surface through systematic hardening
landingpage.containersImgAlt: Open Elements delivers official hardened container images for the German public administration
landingpage.modelTitle: More Than Just Support: Our Model
landingpage.modelP1: Support & Care works differently from traditional vendor support. Together with us, you share the ongoing maintenance and improvement efforts for the supported open source components — openly, transparently and measurably.
landingpage.modelSubtitle: Support & Care follows three important principles:
landingpage.modelPrinciple1: Funds flow directly to the maintainers: Instead of layering superficial support on top, we invest in the vitality of each project's core. The people who actually maintain the code, provide security updates and develop new features are paid directly.
landingpage.modelPrinciple2: Your priorities in the roadmaps: Customer requirements are actively integrated into the development roadmaps of the supported projects. This way, enhancements directly reflect real business needs.
landingpage.modelPrinciple3: Proactive communication: You are not only informed when problems arise, but continuously kept up to date on relevant developments:
landingpage.modelBullet2: Planned API or major version changes
landingpage.modelBullet3: Recommendations for version updates and dependency cleanup
landingpage.modelBullet4: Trends and risks in the OSS ecosystem
landingpage.modelHighlight: Unused support hours do not expire — they flow directly into the further development of the open source components. Every subscription strengthens the projects you rely on.
landingpage.modelP2: We deliver flexible service models for sustainable security. Choose the model that fits your requirements in availability, compliance and SLA.
landingpage.whyP1: We are the maintainers — not just consultants: Our team members are not external consultants who first need to get to know the projects. They are the people who maintain, develop and co-shape these projects within the foundations.
landingpage.whyP2: Open Elements is a well-known and active member of the open source community, contributing not only on a technical level but also in leadership roles across many open source foundations:
landingpage.eclipseDesc: We hold a seat on the Eclipse Foundation Board and are active members of working groups such as Eclipse Adoptium, Eclipse JakartaEE and the ORC WG.
landingpage.lfDesc: We are an active member of the Linux Foundation and contribute to projects and working groups such as Linux Foundation Decentralized Trust, Hiero, and the Agentic AI Foundation.
landingpage.asfDesc: We are active contributors to projects within the Apache Software Foundation, including Apache Maven, Apache Log4j, and Apache Commons.
landingpage.whyHighlight: Open source — done right. Our revenue from Support & Care flows directly into the supported open source projects.
landingpage.faq1Q: Is Support & Care only for Apache Maven?
landingpage.faq1A: No. Support & Care covers five business-critical Java foundational components: Eclipse Temurin, Apache Maven, JUnit, Apache Log4j and Apache Commons. The program started in 2024 with Maven and has been continuously expanded since then.
landingpage.faq2A: Committers and maintainers of the respective open source projects — the people who actually write and maintain the code. No downstream support team, but direct access to the experts.
landingpage.faq3Q: What happens with my subscription fee?
landingpage.faq3A: The revenue flows transparently and traceably into the supported open source projects: payment of maintainers, security updates, bugfixes, documentation and infrastructure.
landingpage.faq4Q: Do I have to subscribe to all five components?
landingpage.faq4A: Get in touch — we tailor the offering to your specific requirements.
landingpage.faq5Q: Does Support & Care help with CRA compliance?
landingpage.faq5A: Yes. Support & Care addresses key CRA requirements: vulnerability monitoring, patch times, documentation, SBOM and long-term maintainability. Prospectively, we also support CRA-compliant attestations.
landingpage.faq6Q: In which languages is support provided?
landingpage.faq6A: German and English — for helpdesk requests as well as workshops, consulting and documentation.
landingpage.faq7Q: What is the difference to framework support (e.g. Spring Boot)?
landingpage.faq7A: Framework support covers the middle layer of your software stack. Support & Care covers the foundational layer underneath: runtime, build tools, logging, testing and utility libraries. Both complement each other — Log4Shell showed that framework support alone is not enough.
landingpage.ctaTitle: Secure the Foundation of Your Java Applications
landingpage.ctaP1: Let us discuss together how Support & Care can protect your software supply chain. Whether private sector or public administration — we will find the right model for you.
landingpage.footnote1: Unused support hours expire monthly and flow into the further development of the supported projects.
landingpage.footnote2: Business days excluding public holidays in NRW.
landingpage.footnote4: Experts are committers and maintainers of the supported OSS projects.
landingpage.footnote5: Webinars and calls via video conference.
